report.name: BCC_Li_Lattice_Constant_Final_Report
report.overall_goal: Calculate the lattice constant of BCC Li using density functional theory with the PBE exchange-correlation functional. The calculation involves: (1) DFT parameter convergence tests to determine optimal kspacing and ecutwfc settings; (2) equation of state calculations at multiple lattice scales using the optimal parameters; (3) EOS fitting to extract the equilibrium lattice constant that minimizes the total energy.

reasons.jobFilenames:
Context: Production run for BCC Li lattice constant calculation using PBE functional. This tool call performs EOS fitting on the 5 completed DFT calculations to extract the equilibrium lattice constant.

Rationale: These 5 EOS calculation output files represent the completed DFT calculations for BCC Li at different lattice scales (0.95, 0.975, 1.0, 1.025, 1.05) around the experimental lattice constant of 3.49 Angstrom. Each file was generated using the production template (ref: o7vvzth0) with optimal converged DFT parameters (kspacing=0.2 Angstrom^-1 from ref i10gdmuz, ecutwfc=40.0 Ry from ref d1hokozn) and PBE functional. The files are referenced by their generation artifact IDs (diyzfwd5, mbt24bik, nw6i5led, qzoy1q39, 9joagbss) which trace back through the EOS test generation (finished job list ref: 0wlhbx9l). The calculate_lc tool will extract total energies from these output files, fit them to an equation of state (e.g., Birch-Murnaghan), and determine the optimal lattice constant that minimizes the total energy. This is the final step in the lattice constant calculation workflow for BCC Li.

reasons.input_file_name:
Context: EOS calculation for BCC Li lattice constant determination using PBE functional. This generates a series of scf calculations at different lattice constants (scaled from the experimental value 3.49 Angstrom) to obtain total energies for equation of state fitting.

Rationale: This parameter specifies the production template Li_bcc_production_template.pwi (ref: o7vvzth0) which incorporates the optimal DFT parameters from convergence tests: ecutwfc=40.0 Ry (ref: d1hokozn) and kspacing=0.2 Angstrom^-1 (ref: i10gdmuz). This template serves as the base for generating EOS calculations, ensuring all EOS points use consistent, converged DFT settings. The template uses the BCC Li structure (ref: d3n8p9u3) with experimental lattice constant 3.49 Angstrom as the starting point.
reasons.stepSize:
Context: EOS calculation for BCC Li lattice constant determination using PBE functional. This generates a series of scf calculations at different lattice constants (scaled from the experimental value 3.49 Angstrom) to obtain total energies for equation of state fitting.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 0.025, which means the cell will be scaled from (1-2*0.025)=0.95 to (1+2*0.025)=1.05 of the initial lattice constant. This gives a range of approximately 3.32 to 3.66 Angstrom around the experimental value of 3.49 Angstrom. The stepSize of 0.025 is a standard choice that provides sufficient data points (typically 5 points: 0.95, 0.975, 1.0, 1.025, 1.05) for accurate EOS fitting while keeping computational cost reasonable. This range should adequately bracket the DFT-PBE predicted lattice constant, which may differ slightly from the experimental value due to functional approximations.

Rationale: This parameter specifies the element Li for the BCC Li lattice constant calculation. Li is the only element in the system, and this value is fixed throughout the entire study.
reasons.ppfiles:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter specifies the PBE pseudopotential li_pbe_v1.4.uspp.F.UPF (ref: x8h5zx4r) for Li. The pseudopotential was identified at the beginning of the study and is held fixed for all calculations to ensure consistency. The PBE functional is required by the study objective.
reasons.filename:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter names the production template file Li_bcc_production_template.pwi. This template will be used by generate_eos_test to create EOS calculation input files with varying lattice constants. The name clearly indicates its purpose as a production template with optimal parameters.
reasons.inputAtomsDir:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter specifies the initial BCC Li structure Li-bcc.xyz (ref: d3n8p9u3) with experimental lattice constant 3.49 Angstrom. This structure was created at the beginning of the study and serves as the base structure for EOS calculations, which will scale it to probe different lattice constants.
reasons.ensembleCalculation:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to false because this is a standard DFT calculation for EOS, not a BEEF ensemble calculation. The study requires determining the lattice constant through EOS fitting, which uses standard scf calculations.
reasons.calculation:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 'scf' for self-consistent field calculation. EOS calculations require scf calculations at different lattice constants to obtain total energies for fitting the equation of state. This is the appropriate calculation type for production EOS runs.
reasons.restart_mode:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 'from_scratch' as each EOS calculation will start fresh from the scaled structure. This ensures clean, independent calculations at each lattice constant point without carrying over information from previous runs.
reasons.prefix:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter sets the output file prefix to 'Li_bcc'. This provides clear identification of the calculation system and will be used consistently across all EOS calculations.
reasons.disk_io:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 'none' to minimize disk I/O operations. For production EOS calculations, we only need the final total energy, so intermediate wavefunctions do not need to be saved to disk, improving computational efficiency.
reasons.ibrav:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 0 to use conventional cell specification via CELL_PARAMETERS card. This is the standard practice in this workflow to avoid ambiguity and ensure proper cell scaling in EOS calculations.
reasons.ecutwfc:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 40.0 Ry, the optimal value determined from the ecutwfc convergence test (ref: d1hokozn). This is the cheapest plane-wave cutoff that achieves 1 meV/atom accuracy compared to the reference calculation (100 Ry), and is now used for production EOS calculations.
reasons.ecutrho:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 160.0 Ry, which is 4 times ecutwfc (40.0 Ry). This is the standard ratio for charge density cutoff in USPP calculations, ensuring sufficient accuracy for charge density representation while maintaining consistency with the optimal ecutwfc.
reasons.occupations:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 'smearing' because BCC Li is metallic and requires smearing to handle partial occupancies at the Fermi level. This setting was established during convergence testing and is maintained for production runs.
reasons.smearing:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 'methfessel-paxton' smearing, which is appropriate for metallic systems like BCC Li. This choice was made during convergence testing and provides good balance between accuracy and convergence stability for metals.
reasons.degauss:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is held fixed at 0.02 Ry (approximately 0.27 eV) for all production EOS calculations. This value was established during convergence testing as a standard choice for metallic systems that provides good balance between accuracy and convergence stability. By keeping degauss constant across all EOS calculations, we ensure that variations in total energy arise solely from changes in lattice constant, not from smearing effects.
reasons.conv_thr:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 1e-6 for the SCF convergence threshold. This is the standard tight convergence criterion for production calculations, ensuring high accuracy in total energies needed for reliable EOS fitting and lattice constant determination.
reasons.electron_maxstep:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 200 to allow sufficient SCF iterations for convergence. This generous limit ensures that all EOS calculations can converge properly, which is critical for obtaining accurate total energies for lattice constant determination.
reasons.kspacing:
Context: Production template for EOS calculation to determine BCC Li lattice constant using PBE functional. This template incorporates the optimal DFT parameters determined from convergence tests and will be used to generate EOS test input files.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 0.2 Angstrom^-1, the optimal value determined from the kspacing convergence test (ref: i10gdmuz). This is the cheapest k-point sampling density that achieves 1 meV/atom accuracy compared to the reference calculation (0.1 Angstrom^-1), and is now used for production EOS calculations.
reasons.input_dft:

9joagbss.description: Generated EOS test job at scale 1.05.

reasons.input_file_name:
Context: EOS calculation for BCC Li lattice constant determination using PBE functional. This generates a series of scf calculations at different lattice constants (scaled from the experimental value 3.49 Angstrom) to obtain total energies for equation of state fitting.

Rationale: This parameter specifies the production template Li_bcc_production_template.pwi (ref: o7vvzth0) which incorporates the optimal DFT parameters from convergence tests: ecutwfc=40.0 Ry (ref: d1hokozn) and kspacing=0.2 Angstrom^-1 (ref: i10gdmuz). This template serves as the base for generating EOS calculations, ensuring all EOS points use consistent, converged DFT settings. The template uses the BCC Li structure (ref: d3n8p9u3) with experimental lattice constant 3.49 Angstrom as the starting point.
reasons.stepSize:
Context: EOS calculation for BCC Li lattice constant determination using PBE functional. This generates a series of scf calculations at different lattice constants (scaled from the experimental value 3.49 Angstrom) to obtain total energies for equation of state fitting.

Rationale: This parameter is set to 0.025, which means the cell will be scaled from (1-2*0.025)=0.95 to (1+2*0.025)=1.05 of the initial lattice constant. This gives a range of approximately 3.32 to 3.66 Angstrom around the experimental value of 3.49 Angstrom. The stepSize of 0.025 is a standard choice that provides sufficient data points (typically 5 points: 0.95, 0.975, 1.0, 1.025, 1.05) for accurate EOS fitting while keeping computational cost reasonable. This range should adequately bracket the DFT-PBE predicted lattice constant, which may differ slightly from the experimental value due to functional approximations.
